steps.title: Read the signs
steps.content: The scatterplot trends downward from left to right, so the slope should be negative. The trend is above the origin near the $y$-axis, so the $y$-intercept should be positive.

steps.title: Overlay the choices
steps.content: Overlay the four answer-choice lines on the scatterplot. Only $y=-1.9x+10.1$ has both the negative slope and positive intercept that match the data.

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: The most appropriate linear model is $y=-1.9x+10.1$, which matches choice B.
